full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding whether MSVMpack satisfies Part A paper selection constraints.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: MSVMpack: A Multi-Class Support Vector Machine Package read all the point carefully in sanchit pdf and this is my selected research paper , i want the good prompt
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped me verify that the paper is published in JMLR (CORE A*), is from 2011, and is based on classical SVM methodology. It also helped generate analytical prompts aligned with rubric requirements.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I manually checked the publication year (2011) and confirmed JMLR is a CORE A* venue. I also verified that the main contribution is methodological (multi-class SVM framework) and not a dataset/system paper.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Feasibility and reproducibility assessment on CPU-only laptop.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: If I want to reproduce a simplified experiment of MSVMpack on a CPU-only laptop using scikit-learn, how can I approximate the Crammer-Singer M-SVM model? What limitations will I face compared to MSVMpack?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Clarified that LinearSVC with multi_class='crammer_singer' approximates the joint optimization model and explained limitations such as lack of kernelization and absence of dual Frank-Wolfe optimization.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I cross-checked scikit-learn documentation and confirmed that LinearSVC supports Crammer-Singer formulation. I understood that scikit-learn solves primal optimization, unlike MSVMpack’s dual Frank-Wolfe method.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding theoretical formulation of generic M-SVM.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Explain the generic formulation of Multi-Class SVM given in Definition 1 of MSVMpack (2011). Break down each term in the objective function and constraints intuitively and mathematically.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped me understand the objective function components such as the penalization term, regularization parameter lambda, and structured constraints across classes.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I re-read Definition 1 from the paper and matched each explanation with the equations provided. I ensured that I understood how the margin constraints differ from binary SVM.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Comparative understanding across multi-class SVM variants.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: How does the generic M-SVM formulation unify the models of Weston & Watkins, Crammer & Singer, Lee et al., and MSVM2? Explain what changes in parameters (M, p, K1, K2, K3) and how that affects optimization and classification behavior.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped me understand how parameter variations produce different known M-SVM formulations and how the generic model acts as a unifying framework.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I examined Table 1 in the paper and manually matched each configuration with the description in the response. I verified that changes in p and matrix M influence the loss structure.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Identifying failure cases and limitations for deeper understanding.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Under what conditions can Multi-Class SVM fail or perform poorly? Consider class imbalance, high Q (number of classes), kernel choice, and margin geometry.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped me think critically about computational complexity for large Q, sensitivity to kernel selection, and potential degradation under class imbalance.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I connected these points to the discussion in Section 3.1 about computational burden increasing when Q > 20 and reflected on SVM limitations from class notes.

top_5_prompts.prompt: If I want to reproduce a simplified experiment of MSVMpack on a CPU-only laptop using scikit-learn, how can I approximate the Crammer-Singer M-SVM model? What limitations will I face compared to MSVMpack?
top_5_prompts.why_important: This prompt directly addressed feasibility constraints of Part A and ensured that the selected paper can be reasonably approximated on a standard laptop.

top_5_prompts.prompt: How does the generic M-SVM formulation unify the models of Weston & Watkins, Crammer & Singer, Lee et al., and MSVM2? Explain what changes in parameters (M, p, K1, K2, K3) and how that affects optimization and classification behavior.
top_5_prompts.why_important: Helped me understand the core theoretical contribution of the paper — the unification of multiple multi-class SVM formulations.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Explain the generic formulation of Multi-Class SVM given in Definition 1 of MSVMpack (2011). Break down each term in the objective function and constraints intuitively and mathematically.
top_5_prompts.why_important: Improved my mathematical understanding of the optimization objective and constraints, which is critical for Part C.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Under what conditions can Multi-Class SVM fail or perform poorly? Consider class imbalance, high Q (number of classes), kernel choice, and margin geometry.
top_5_prompts.why_important: Encouraged deeper analytical thinking about limitations and real-world behavior, aligning with rubric emphasis on failure cases.

top_5_prompts.prompt: MSVMpack: A Multi-Class Support Vector Machine Package read all the point carefully in sanchit pdf and this is my selected research paper , i want the good prompt
top_5_prompts.why_important: Initiated structured analysis of paper validity, venue verification, and alignment with examination constraints.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Part A.
